review_text: Simple to set up. This landline connection is interfaced with and Dependent on cell towers for transmission of signal. This is an indication of the obsolescence of hard wired equipment available for secure and more dependable phone service. The old technology was and still is greatly appreciated, but has largely been replaced by cell phone and cell tower repeaters to relay the communications (antenna) streams. The changeover to internet providers for regular phone service, commercial, industrial, and small businesses are not excluded. This COMMUNITY PHONE is Not a party line, which certainly would be considered a totally UNSECURED LINE for personal and/or private communications. This Community Phone requires a 120VAC outlet to power this unit continuously. When power is lost, you can expect service to continue. This service will switch automatically to BATTERY BACKUP! Battery life has not been reported in the setup instructions and list of components in the package when shipped to the customers. I lost power and the phone worked just fine. Note: You must purchase a landline handset for service upon installation. The regular phone jacks for landlines still are the connection ports for signal incoming and the handset to make or receive phone calls. Still assessing service quality while speaking. review_title: EICR check carried out through Romans
review_text: ARK was asked to carry an EICR check on my rented property by Roman's estate agent, as they are their approved contractor. The visit on 02.07.20 highlighted a change in the consumer unit and rated this C1/C2 which is in a potentially dangerous condition (522.6.202; 522.6.203) (You can only have one rating and not 2) at a cost of £480.00. Replacing the smoke alarms and a damaged double socket were correct at the time. They are members of NICEIC but use the NAPIT guidelines to code items that should be a C3 to a C1/C2 rating, revenue generation or what. I had to quickly go through the guidelines for NICEIC and get a reputable contractor in, as the flat is 12 years old and only required a modification was a RCD to improve the C3 rating.Thanks for your standard response and yes I am a responsible landlord, which is why I got the EICR check carried out by an honest electrician. The visit on 02.07.20 highlighted a change in the consumer unit and rated this C1/C2 which is in a potentially dangerous condition (522.6.202; 522.6.203) (You can only have one rating and not 2) at a cost of £480.00. Replacing the smoke alarms and a damaged double socket were correct at the time. They are members of NICEIC but use the NAPIT guidelines to code items that should be a C3 to a C1/C2 rating, revenue generation or what. I had to quickly go through the guidelines for NICEIC and get a reputable contractor in, as the flat is 12 years old and only required a modification was a RCD to improve the C3 rating.Thanks for your standard response and yes I am a responsible landlord, which is why I got the EICR check carried out by an honest electrician. The government guidelines allows unscrupulous companies to manipulate the guidelines, to suit themselves and am sure that landlords are also being scammed into doing a lot more work than is required.

review_text: We have had so many issues that we would never buy off Linden again.There were so many snagging issues and things that weren't put right. As soon as the showhome office closed all support ceased and emails from us or any of the neighbouring houses were ignored.It is worth noting that my husbands previous new build, with a different provider, didn't have a single problem. name: Ultimate Ears
review_text: UE Megaboom 3 is a disappointment. In spite of the marketing on Ultimate Ears Website with terms such as "Way more boom" and "Thundering bass" and trying various equalizer settings on the Official App, the "Megaboom 3" Speaker objectively has less bass response (particularly lower end bass) and subjectively poorer sound quality (in terms of clarity and definition) than my old Ultimate Ears Boombox purchased in 2013.Given the disparity between the marketing claims, the online reviews -vs- my own experience. I can't help thinking that some of the glowing video reviews over the internet about the Megaboom 3 have possibly been paid for as part of possible astroturfing campaign. We will probably never know.The speaker has been returned to the retailer for a full refund. In the process, another customer over heard the conversation between me and the store rep only to affirm that he too was disappointed at the lack of bass and which he likewise returned as well (with another retailer).Unfortunately, due to the gravity of the situation, I'm in no position to consider nor recommend any UE branded products going forward and it has adversely affected my trust and regard toward the parent brand, Logitech.

I also get email alerts that a fax has either been received or sent. Although the rates to increase from time to time, MetroFax is easy to work with and will work with you to maintain a rate that works for you. I have been very pleased with this service. Their customer service is great also. Although I prefer to speak with a human being, they only now offer chat support. No worries! My recent chat with Lyra went smoothly and I still felt that same level of personal support that I would have had we been speaking on the phone. review_title: Bad financial advice led to lose of property deposit
review_text: An agent who currently works here strongly recommended a financial investment and promised it was 100% safe and secure, and he knew lots of people invested in it and was very pushy to sell it. Avoid, or at least be very careful, what advice you take. Financial advisors are only interested in the commission they make from selling you financial products. They have no interest helping you, or taking account of individual circumstances. Once there is a problem, they suddenly vanish. They take their commission and you are left with less money than before you met them.

review_title: I had a car insurance with NRMA
review_text: I had a car insurance with NRMA. Recently I moved interstate. While my car was in transit, I cancelled the comprehensive car insurance (but still have the CTP with NRMA) because I didn't know when the car would arrive as it may take 1-3 months due to covid. It took 40 days (30 days in transit + almost 2 weeks at the custom for clearance) to arrive. So when the car arrived, I renewed my comprehensive car insurance with NRMA. However, the insurance price was higher than before. I called NRMA and they said that I lost all of my loyalty discounts which I accumulated over the years and am now starting the insurance as a new customer. I thought the no-claim bonus and loyalty discounts would be on my profile and even I renew the car insurance, they would still apply. Not very happy with them. I spoke to NRMA as well as SGIO (which NRMA subcontracted to in Western Australia), but both said I had to purchase the insurance from scratch. They couldn't do anything about it.

I noticed that , when 2 pair win the hand, the winner got their 2 pair on the first 2 cards of the flop 80% of the time. Also, when a pair shows up on the board, a full house wins 75% of the time. But my number 1 gripe comes with the extremely high number of Full houses that I see during a 2-3 hour period. I see more Straight flushes and quads than I've seen in my entire 80 year life, playing real cards at real tables. I notice an inordinate number of wins under strange circumstances by the player in the the 1 o'clock position. Also, note the high number of times you land on the grey "lowest" amounts on the spinning wheel, when trying to increase your chips when logging in. There are only 6 "GREY(low)" amounts out of about 12 total slots, but when you spin, it will land on the "GREY" 80% of the time. The odds just are just  CROOKED!

review_text: Oakham Overlap Shed 10 x 7 Review Update So ILikeToSell have finally replaced the shed floor after a lot of discussion and providing pictures of shed floor rot to the OSB board. But thankyou to the customer service assistant for your help.

name: The Kitchen Depot
review_title: Great value for money, great service - what’s not to love?
review_text: We bought our kitchen at the end of 2018 from the Kitchen Depot.Firstly, the service in the showroom at Hillington was fantastic. They did all of the cad drawing, sent people out to take measurements, and at no point did it feel like a hard sell. The staff also very helpfully recommended websites to buy matching white goods for the kitchen - they were very knowledgable. Kitchen Depots are NOT overly cheap but this does mean they LAST. They offer amazing value for money, and their base doors and fittings are far more sturdy than the likes of in style or Howdens, who were the local competition. Helpfully, the kitchen depot offer finance too, and this is at a fantastic rate of interest, making getting a new kitchen affordable. Another top thing about this company is that they do all the fitting/trades at the same time. Our kitchen was a wee bit more complex as we were removing walls and replastering ceilings - this meant it took a bit longer than it should have, but the folk did a fantastic job with the fit and they really took their time and were incredibly helpful in answering our questions. We have had one or two issues with the kitchen over the last 3 and a half years, but the company have always been there to fix the teething issues and nothing is ever a hassle. Even recently, when I contacted the company after chipping the paint on my door, they sent out a free tester pot for me to touch it up. I cannot recommend them highly enough. The kitchen has been fanatic value for money for us, and everyone always says ‘wow’ when they enter our home, nearly four years on. What’s not to love?

name: Discogs
review_title: DISCOGS USELESS. ANDRÉ KOLE A LIAR AND RIP OFF MERCHANT
review_text: I don't know why Discogs offer a 'support' procedure as they are as useful as they are as supportive as a chocolate jock strap. Utterly useless and set up to favour crooks. I ordered a NM graded LP from André Kole in the Netherlands for a total of €38.40. NM (as set out in the grading instructions within Discogs site) basically means the record LOOKS unplayed and as new. What arrived looked like the LPs had been unsleeved and stored under a bed for twenty years and the sleeve stored in direct sunlight for same given its bleached condition. I questioned Kole stating my unhappiness. He disagreed and reckoned the grading was down to personal opinion. I knew I was definitely dealing with an idiot. I wanted a return and refund. He reluctantly agreed, also stating I wouldn't be out of pocket. I spent a further £14.10 to return tracked (I wasn't risking Kole saying it hadn't arrived). In short, the LP hit customs in Europe and stayed there for over TWO MONTHS. By the time it eventually arrived, the Discogs feedback window CLOSED - something they claim they cannot change - and I know Kole was counting on this happening by this point. He did refund me €30 but reneged on his promise not to leave me out of pocket. Now over £20 in shipping. Kole now not responding and Discogs not interested.AVOID DISCOGS IF POSSIBLE AND DO NOT BUY FROM ANDRÉ KOLE IN NETHERLANDS.

name: Discogs
review_text: BUYER BEWAREThey say that there is buyer protection - in reality this depends on how you pay and there is little Discogs can do should your order have an issue. The story:I bought a vinyl from a reseller for an album that was released the week before. I hadn't received it after a couple of months, the seller told me that there were issues on the release of the vinyl due to distribution lines, COVID, etc. They told me they could prove they had made the order and that the vinyl would be shipped directly to me when it's ready. After 7 months of constant contact with the seller wondering where my £100 vinyl was I contacted Discogs who told me in a long winded way that there's nothing they can do to get my money back if the time had expired from PayPal, and I couldn't leave feedback because they have an arbitrary 90 day policy on leaving feedback on a seller. At 90 days post payment the seller was telling me that it would be shipped within a month, my money had been sent, the seller had not updated the page to payment received. All very suspicious. LUCKILY the seller was legitimate, I got the vinyl 7 months after paying for it. But had I not, there's nothing Discogs would have done to help me get my money back.

review_text: Worst support ever. This review reflects my experiences with the site and their putrid staff members rather than the traditional pros/ cons/ best bang for your buck review, either way, it still matters just the same. For two years the staff removed every negative review I left for a seller whenever it was appropriate. Sellers got away with everything and the staff repeatedly left me with no recourse. Last time they did it, naturally I became enraged and I had had enough so righteous indignation took front row seat  and I felt compelled enough to lash out and issue their staff the worst 2 year cursing out they've likely ever gotten in their whole lives, and they deserved it. They then blocked access to everything in an attempt to retaliate on top of the 2 years they participated in the discrimination. Now the block is not a problem for me, there are tons of workarounds. Nonetheless they still to this day on a brand new account continue to engage in this childish game of cat and mouse where they've seemed to focus their efforts  into non-stop spying on my network activity (instead of doing their jobs) and blocking every new account, new IP address I come up with. And they've been doing this nonstop, painstakingly for weeks. It makes them look desperate, misanthropic, empty, vitriolic, pathetic. I've also recently found out they've removed hundreds of negative feedback from a seller, just look up in their forum under thread 718917. For over 2 years I put up with their constant attempts to marginalize and push me into a corner every time I had a problem. The marketplace is a joke. Feedback system is a joke. Worst support ever.

review_title: I've purchased from this company before…
review_text: I've purchased from this company before many times. Some products are amazing, some really overpriced especially their small perfume bottles, others not so good and others not good at all. Ok, why the two stars? I asked for samples of several perfumes to be sent to me only to be shipped and billed all the perfumes. The reason I am asking for samples is to see if I like the perfumes in order to buy them because it's dumb to send a customer a brand new perfume for them to open it smell and return it if they don't like it. That's wasted items there. Another thing I have found really annoying is that items that smelled amazing are no longer available for purchase and thirdly, why would you issue a cheque and send it via post to a customer in 2021? The customer service is ok in that they processed a refund but via cheque? Come on. Didn't even ask me if this was my preferred method of refund. Then to be sent whole bottles of perfumes and then having to return them cus the agent didn't understand what a sample is. After weighing the pros and the cons of dealing with this company I don't think I'll ever buy from here again. Their marketing is really good they always catch me with their promotions but the items I like are never there. Til I see the items I like, I'll never come back here again.

name: Smallburn Cottages
review_title: The Arches - A Truly 5 Star House
review_text: The Arches is truly a 5 star house. We usually use Premier Cottages as their standard is always very high, but they didn't have accommodation in the High Newton/Low Newton area, and I am so glad that we found The Arches. Our family have spent a wonderful week there. The accommodation was excellent in every way - it was spotlessly clean and the fact that each bedroom had its own bathroom made it perfect for our family of my wife and I, our son and daughter-in-law and two granddaughters. We were slightly concerned that there wasn't a garden, and although it stated in the literature that the house was dog-friendly (and this was one of the reasons for booking) we were worried that there would be nowhere for the dog to be outside. That did not prove to be a problem as the gravelled area at the front of the house was perfect for the dog, and in any case the beach is only a 5 minute walk away. Another perfect thing about the house is that its next-door-neighbour is The Joiners Arms and we spent a good few afternoons sitting outside the pub enjoying a drink after our long walks. The food there is also excellent and cannot be recommended highly enough (especially the take-away fish and chips).

review_title: Ghosted and dropped - don't waste your time
review_text: I am a first time buyer and I don't recommend using John Charcol - it was a very stressful experience. Things were great at the beginning and they offered me a really good deal with a high lending amount and little interest rate, which suited my needs. As I was house hunting at the time, I based my price range on this quote, and as it took me a few months to find somewhere, they kept updating the same quote but in line with the current interest rates. When I finally found somewhere within that price range, they said they could no longer give me that quote, despite it being the same for months, and were offering me a lending amount far lower than the original, with a higher interest rate. When trying to resolve the situation in order to save the property I had offered, my broker ghosted me for 3 days, and then never came back to me on the situation. I think with first time buyers they offer a great quote originally in the hopes that it will get cleared once an offer has been accepted, however you are left in the lurch if this doesn't go to plan and then they just drop you. Save yourself the time and stress and use somewhere else.

name: CircuitMess
review_title: Fantastic idea for an engaging STEM DIY project.
review_text: Bought the Nibble (on sale). The company communicated well and shipped the product in a reasonable timeframe. They seem genuinely passionate about their products.The Nibble has been a great DIY project for my 10yo son to learn some skills like soldering and troubleshooting. He's always wanted to build his own handheld console, so I couldn't believe this was actually a thing. He learnt some patience, resilience and the joy of completing a project. What a great idea from CicuitMess.Of course we didn't have to build the entire thing from scratch. The kit was simple enough that it didn't take months to assemble, and we could see the end goal early on. It wasn't overwhelming for a kid.Now he's super keen to try the next phase which is the coding of your own games using CircuitBlocks which looks similar to Scratch, which he's familiar with. Then after that there is the levelled up coding phase using C++ and/or Python, which will give him some advanced skills to pursue a career or hobby.A truly fantastic idea for kids interested in this sort of thing.

name: CircuitMess
review_text: I did a lot of research before subscribing to CircuitMess, simply because electronics kits can be costly.  This has turned out to be a really good buy.  My 12 year old daughter, who I did not expect to have an interest, puts them together herself with no help.  My 8 year old son requires help with the soldering but can otherwise handle the building.  We’re all learning the programming piece together.  The website and instructions are really user friendly.It’s a lot of fun to actually be able to build a device that you can actually use.  I know many kits are no-solder, but that honestly is the most fun and challenging part of it.  It forces the kids to pay attention, because it requires a little extra work if you mess up.  We are all really impressed with the thought that goes into the kits and the kids ask when we’ll get the next one.

name: Shutterfly
review_text: I have made several Shutterfly books before. Reading several comments regarding their issues with using Shutterfly, I understand their complaints. I haven't had as much problems with the crashing of the site. But adding photos can be problematic to say the least. I have never found Shutterfly to be user friendly. That being said I haven't had a lot of problems after practice. However, my most recent project was delivered to me this past week. Even though I ordered the same quality of paper to be used as I have in the past, the quality was not up to par. It was distinctively a lower quality - the pages were noticeably thinner and less glossy. Shutterfly is not taking ownership of this. They claim the paper is the same it always has been. They did offer to redo the book for me; however, I doubt that would change anything. I also strongly agree with the comments regarding the pricing of their books. Even though the project contained less pages and the paper was less quality I spent a LOT more than I have before for same-sized books. Of course, this is partially because I got better 'sales' with the other projects. Shutterfly - forget these sales gimmicks and just offer a regular affordable pricing. I think I will be looking elsewhere for my next book.

name: The Colonnade Hotel
review_text: This hotel is awful, really disappointing! Faulty Towers would have been better!My husband and I stayed at The Colonnade Hotel at the end of November. We work hard and so were really looking forward to a trip away to London. The photos on the website do not reflect how tired the decor and furniture really are. The room we stayed in was supposed to be a deluxe room and it was far from it! The net curtains had mould, the shower didn't work properly, the drinking glasses were dirty and the room was cold during the night. We went to the restaurant the first night we arrived as we didn't get to the hotel until around 7pm. The starter of hummus and flatbread came to our table as hummus and a few rounds on plain toast. The main meal was chicken, rice and salad was all they had on offer, all other meals on the menu were unavailable. The chicken was not hot and there was gristle in the chicken. I could not eat the food and so we left to get pub food. The key card did not always work on the bedroom door and so we were afraid of leaving our belongings unattended. This was a Groupon deal that cost over £300 for two nights including two evening meals and breakfast each morning. I could not stomach going back to the restaurant and so we left the hotel on the last morning feeling like we had been conned out of our money. Could not recommend this hotel to anyone, totally ruined our trip to London!

Okay, you need to do the Anti-Money Laundering verification, love it or hate it, it's the standard in quite a few places. Whether this process specifically is actually required by law- I'm unsure, because a few other bigger websites for similar purposes do not implement this procedure.So you sold your skin, it leaves your inventory, you have to upload your ID and selfies, wait up to 48h, and then you get the money, after the 5% fee? Oh no no, then you have to PAY ANOTHER 5$ WITH ANOTHER COMISSION FEE ON TOP OF THE 5%? Yeah, no way that's not a 1 star experience. You're legally twisting your customers' arms, after you draw them in, leaving them no choice but to take it.P.S. Update- and you cannot withdraw the 5$. Ok.P.P.S. And there's fees on withdrawal.

All accompanying components i.e. including two 2-channel remotes per door and separate internal wall control panels with build in illumination -again robust and high quality. Was given a likely and early installation date which was honoured. Certainly, this is one of the best experiences I have had from a commercial organisation. We were kept informed throught the process. My strong recommendation is, if you are considering purchasing a roller garage door, you should ALWAYS get a quote from these guys. review_text: We ordered our tiles after Tile Expert gave us an estimated delivery date early June. Well in time for our fitting end of June/ start July. Now we are FORCED to change the design of our bathroom because these guys messed it up. They didn’t seem to understand the importance of timelines and how not having the product totally destroys an install timeline. If they had kept me informed that would be fair enough but they didn’t and kept telling me it had been delivered.

review_title: Payroll marked my clock out short.
review_text: I am aware that pay slips can get mixed up however we have supervisors who sign us in and out and write specific clock in and clock out times. Last weekend working at Wembley was horrendous. A lot of staff were rude and ill mannered. Job card said I’d finish at 23:30 and I have to ask to leave at 00:00 where the man kindly signed me out, wrote 00:00 clock out as I remember showing him the time and he kept the job cards. I got home at 3am the next morning due to missing the last train I was scheduled to get. I also got to work before 15:30 as asked an hour before (although my shift time was 15:45). I am happy to be flexible and take the longer journey home for work but not when my job card will mark my clock out time any earlier than I actually did. The work demanded a lot of me and I want my job cards to be accurate. 23:40 instead of 00:00 is only 20 mins, I don’t know if it affects my pay, but it affected my cost of travel and time to get home. I hope this gets sorted fairly.

review_text: Booked a Renault Twingo for my wife and I in Slovenia. I did this online via the uk site, charged me £58 for 4 days. Which is excellent value. I took out the min insurance, shouldn't have but I did. The pick up was ok, bloke was a little stern, but feel it was a language barrier thing and/or Christmas Eve thing. was told on the email I received the deposit would be £290 but he wanted €700, approx £550. Erm.. well I paid it and we were off, bit confused by the sticker thing, where you could drive anywhere, didn't realise he meant it paid for tolls, until our rep at the hotel told us. Car was good, did its job. Returned 4 days later, when through all the hand car back it bit. All ok, when does the block come off my credit card? Automatically, was the answer. ok thanks. Get back to England and after reading the reviews on TrustPilot started pooping my pants. Credit card company said wait until 31/12/2015 when it was meant to come off and it did!!!  I was really worried about not getting my money back but so far it has been ok. Maybe its just the Slovenian company that are very good, I dont know. Anyway, pleased all round. Must push their rating up a little bit I suppose.

name: Tempest Hosting, LLC.
review_title: Inaccurate Delivery Times, Awful Support, Incompetent System Engineers
review_text: Inaccurate Delivery Times, Awful Support, Incompetent System EngineersThe only reason I give them 2 stars is because of their Path.net DDoS protection. That's the only positive aspect I've experienced from Tempest.Firstly, the support. I waited for a response on my ticket for over 36 days. It was a simple request for a BGP announcement. Tempest has a major lack of competent staff members, which leads to crazy long wait times. I'm not the only customer waiting months for support, I've heard numerous Tempest.net horror stories. I ended up giving up on waiting for my support ticket to be completed and they refunded me, at least it was a simple process to get refunded.Delivery times have been a major issue for Tempest, there is a huge lack of in-stock servers means you can be waiting a few months to year for a custom server order delivery especially in certain locations. Tempest has a major issue with hiring, instead of hiring like an actual company would, they resort to hiring in-experienced systems engineers that lack any formal education on completing day-to-day operations a network or system engineer would usually complete. I believe this to be the reason of these numerous horror stories you'll hear coming from Tempest. Zigi is the only staff member to my knowledge that's able to complete tasks with networking such as BGP, that is laughable for a million dollar company like Path / Tempest.Compared to similar hosting companies that can get this task done in 1 day, Tempest takes 36 times longer.EDIT: Tempest has resolved the problem and announced my IP range and added 3 months of free service to my dedicated servers.

review_text: Having recently moved into our new home there was the inevitable snagging list. Barwood do this differently to most in that you have 7-days from completion to report any issues. review_text: Look down the list for the childish names of supposed happy reviewers and make your own mind up. By all means buy one of these houses if you wish to be bitterly disappointed.

review_title: I felt like I was scammed right up until the glasses arrived. Fix your website!
review_text: The entire website looks incredibly shady, so right after I ordered I regretted it as I read the reviews on Thrustpilot. I ordered a Rayban and apparently I made a mistake in entering my email address. Their webstore is quite terrible so it did not check if I entered a valid email. It did however allow me to pay for the glasses. This is bad! Always check if your customer entered a valid email address, or you have no way of contacting them and they won't receive any updates on their order. It's quite possible that most of the bad reviews here are just because of this. People make a mistake, have no way of contacting the seller and so they think it's a scam. I must say that their customer service did respond and the mistake got fixed, but still the entire thing feels shady(heh). They send it through UPS but the receipt says that there are no shipping costs. With UPS? How? It doesn't make sense?The glasses arrived within a reasonable time and they seem real, so that's good. Still, I can only give two stars because the entire process from paying to receiving the glasses felt as a scam. I don't like to be anxious for 3 weeks thinking I lost 160 euros just because the web shop is crap.

name: Gupta Program
review_title: Gupta gave me hope after getting Long Covid
review_text: This is my second review, an update about how everything is going after some additional months of doing the Gupta program. I am doing it after developing Long Covid in March 2020, and I started the Gupta program around a year later, in May 2021. I have decided to repurchase the Gupta program for an additional year because I'm not yet healed - but it helps me tremendously to have the videos and the retraining to keep me going. The program made me find hope that I will heal, and even though I had hoped I would have further in my healing, I've come a long way! The webinars have been  wonderful, especially the interaction with other retrainers - to hear what have helped them and hear about their healing journey. I really appreciate that Ashok has said they're going to be more interactive - I hope we'll get to see more retrainers tell their story - it's amazing to not feel alone. There are a few improvements I'd be happy to see: I'd like to see auto-play for the videos, so the next video would start directly after the other one. The website is quite slow loading the videos, and it's quite a faff,to have to go back from full-screen mode, press next session, go back to full-screen mode again etc.. When being in a flow of watching the videos, auto-play (that stayed in full-screen) would have been appreciated!Secondly, some meditations and exercises don't have the same audio quality as the rest of them. I really like the visualisation exercise, but find it harder to immerse myself into it when the audio quality isn't very good.

name: Gupta Program
review_title: More on my experience with the Gupta Program
review_text: This is an update to a previous review.  I still recommend people to take Ashok Gupta's  program.  It has really helped people with specific disorders and so it is important to have a reasonably accurate diagnosis going into this work.  In my case, much later in life (a couple of years back actually), I learned I had a significant respiratory condition (not just asthma) that impacted my energy level as well as having CFS.  So when I previously used the Gupta Program, my recovery was affected by this permanent lung damage and so it was good to finally know this, and it had a bearing on why I did not make it to 80-100% recovery.  After adding specific breathing modalities from Buteyko Breathing, did I then continue to improve further.  The two modalities of the Gupta Program and Buteyko Breathing are  very helpful for me to stay on an even keel.  Any notable improvement is a success for one undertaking this journey even if one does not get to 100%.

name: Gupta Program
review_title: A well-rounded program - compassionate and deeply healing
review_text: I was referred to this program by an environmental doctor after a mold illness diagnosis. I also suffered from food intolerances and had chronic pain for well over a decade. Understanding that all of these conditions stem from the same root cause - a dysregulated nervous system - was eye-opening. Being given the tools to develop resilience and gradually return to balance has been a real gift of self-empowerment. The book and the webinars are informative and easy to follow. The twelve-week virtual sessions with Ashok have been instrumental and a real lifeline, humanizing a journey that can sometimes feel isolating. Ashok is kind and compassionate. The addition of retrainer testimonials during the sessions has been truly uplifting. At about 60% recovered, I’m well on my way to full health and now, instead of dreading this condition, I look forward to the journey.

name: Gupta Program
review_text: I developed multiple chemical sensitivities and food sensitivities 30 years ago.   Since that time, I have tried my best to manage my symptoms by following a rotation diet, avoiding chemicals and fragrances, and trying a variety of treatment modalities.  These things kept me going, but they brought no hope of healing.  When I heard about the Gupta Program and read the explanation of the condition, I felt great relief as this was the first explanation that made complete sense to me and gave me hope.  I signed up immediately and stayed with it through some other health issues. Recently I have been able to focus exclusively on the program, and have started tolerating more soaps, cleaning chemicals and fabrics.  I still have food sensitivities, but I am sure I have found the right path back to complete health. The program has also helped to bring some healing of past trauma and an increase in self-confidence -- I am deeply grateful for this!  I highly recommend this comprehensive program and the supportive webinars!

review_text: I really appreciate this program as it doesn’t promise to be a quick fix but is really supportive, no matter what your functional ability or life situation. name: Offpeakluxury
review_title: Show no compassion and not helpful
review_text: We were due to stay at Mar Hall hotel near Glasgow on the 28th April 2022 and booked our stay with a gift voucher purchased by our son from offpeakluxury.On the morning of our stay, we came down to find our dog seriously unwell and subsequently had to have him put to sleep later on that morning.We were totally distraught and couldn’t continue with our stay that night.We had also booked flights up that morning from Heathrow to Glasgow for our stay and British Airways were brilliant in issuing us with a voucher to use at a later date, however when I called offpeakluxury to cancel our reservation at Mar Hall they were not so helpful. They showed no compassion and just said that because I was cancelling within 48 hours there was nothing they could do and to check our travel insurance which didn’t cover loss of pet anyway.We didn’t want a refund, just the chance to stay at the hotel another time.They didn’t look at our situation on an individual basis and use any discretion in helping us out at a very upsetting time. After what we went through that morning that would have gone a very long way.Beware booking through them if your plans have to change at the last minute even for something so completely devastating.In response to the reply below,On the morning I lost my dog and had to contact offpeakluxury I was extremely upset and was not able to face a confrontational phone call with the customer service advisor. It has taken me weeks to come to terms with our loss and even consider contacting them again to see if they could reconsider our case. A little more sympathy would have been very nice.

review_text: Just when I began hoping that the obstacle course (dealing with Loberon) is finally ending - another surprise! I had made peace with the fact that some things were not delivered at all, some were held somewhere in the warehouse without anyone notifying me and some arrived broken - things happen and we learn from our mistakes (my decision - never to order from Loberon again). The broken furniture was picked up relatively quickly (had to wait only for a week) and refund was promised. Good? Not really.Last week (a month after broken stuff was picked up) I had a friend call them inquiring about refund. Response was - it will be issued next week. Good? Not really. Today, when I sent an email to enquire about what does the "next week" mean, exactly, I received a response: "Please send the receipt from post office (I never took it there) and list the items (the order number was in the subject line)." The nightmare continues...My credit card company gave me a temporary refund, but as Loberon has been dragging their heals, it will be reversed this week. I have to, once again, find the money to pay for something I have not had a pleasure of owning. I do realize that Loberon's refund will eventually arrive, but... 4 months of headache, 4 months of money being tied down (we are talking 4 figures), 4 months of unanswered emails, 4 months of frustration and stress...I understand the issues some companies have in the wake of Covid, and Loberon is one of them. However, it seems that while most other firms have recovered, Loberon has not.

Although there are no studies claiming that CBD increases appetite or helps with nausea in dogs, Seth suggested we give it a try, it can’t hurt.  Wow are we glad we did!  It’s been two weeks of giving him a treat first thing in the morning 15 min before breakfast, the vomiting stopped the first day and he’s starting to put weight back on.  His blood work came back normal from the vet with slightly elevated white cells so we are working with them to treat inflammation in his GI track and they told us to keep doing what we are doing since he’s gaining.  We’ve upped his treats to 1 before breakfast and again at dinner. We are now on a subscription plan for the treats so we don’t run out.  I can’t thank Seth enough for taking our call.

name: TUI.ch
review_title: We booked one night in Wengen from TUI
review_text: We booked one night in Wengen from TUI,  we booked a very nice upgraded more expensive room with a view and balcony and paid company in full and received a confirmation from TUI the next day with the room that we booked. Upon  arrival at the hotel, hotel informed us they were overbooked and we were downgraded into the smallest room on the attic, no view, small window and no balcony. Hotel informed us that this happened to few TUI customers and there was nothing they could do for us. I contacted TUI the next day to ask for partial refund, they said there is nothing they could do because we did not call them from the hotel instead of dealing with hotel directly behind the scenes. I just want to make sure for any future customers to be aware of their business model and there is no recourse or even attempt to help. We cannot do anything because we prepaid in full, I am just glad it was one night and not a ruined longer vacation. We would never use them again and will go through much more known companies. We travel all the time and this is the first time I had to deal with such blatant lack of recourse. Julia

name: Swift Resolution
review_title: Swift Resolution has been a Game Changer
review_text: Swift Resolution has been a game-changer for our business. We have previously suffered clients who despite being given the service and deliverables did not pay on time,  and some did not at all. We have incurred significant costs by extended credit to companies that are uncredit-worthy and suffered the indignity of having to write off bad debts. I have check online and take the chance to buy the universal fit option (All samsung) with the accessory bar option.Once received, installing the accessery bar option was pretty easy.I got some issue with the casing, not because of the product buyt because of ny phone size with is smaller than the casing, which make it float inside the casing. No big deal, I use the foam that comes with the phone mount, did a beautiful shapre around my phone and put that inside the casing and voila! My phone no longer float and it works great! I have tested on the road and it works fine. I am really happy with this and it worth the money, the quality is really good.

This was not mentioned in Sydney at the time of shipment! So, causing company, me and the receiver in Turkey a big trouble. I have sent through email and also called both +61 37 0356818 and +61 737245700 to resolve the issue yesterday 26-April. name: Dhl Express
review_text: Awful. Was told a total of five times that my package would be delivered “today”. It never was and no one let me know. Each time, I would get a notice a day or two later giving an excuse as to why it wasn’t delivered. Four times, it was that my address was incorrect or “didn’t exist” (I’ve lived here 14 years). Once, it said “delivery couldn’t be attempted because of traffic”. Huh? I would stay home to receive and sign for my package each time. There’s an option when you go into “manage delivery” of having them leave package without a signature. I didn’t dare choose that because it was an expensive item and I had zero faith in DHL at that point. I chose to have it delivered to a DHL “center” and pick it up myself. Several days after I chose that, the DHL driver actually called me to say she was looking for my house and couldn’t find it. What??? They are completely disorganized with little to no interest in customer service. I’m guessing it’s why they failed when they tried to expand to the US years ago. They cannot compete with other carriers. They don’t even come close. I much prefer when overseas sellers arrange for the post office to deliver once a package arrives in the states. It would be delivered much faster by the PO, which is saying something. I still don’t have my package. Just got a notice it’s at the center I choose, but based on the numerous false promises from DHL so far, I’m not feeling confident that it will actually be there when I arrive.

review_text: Pov: you send a package from Sweden to Bulgaria- express delivery at home.The delivery was fast but it did not arrive at home as required, for a silly excuse such as "slippery road". Additionally, before I confirmed my order, i called the Swedish DHL to ask if the package will actually arrive at home and I got the confirmation that it does. All words, no facts.Pov 2: You are supposed to get a package from Bulgaria.Again the delivery does not come at home. This time I could see the courier from my window. She was going back to the truck with several packages in her hand and she soon drove away. Not much late I receive a message that there was no response "when the currier tried to deliver". As far as I could see, the courier did not try particularly hard. The door to the building has a code that the courier clearly did not know. However, what the courier did know was my phone number and my email adress, where I have been receiving the information about the package. Despite that I am obliged to go to a Service Point and get my package there, even though it is paid for delivery AT HOME.Two different delieveries, two different countries, same problem. I do not recommend.
